§6Synthesized Life: Mob Spawners§f

§aExtract DNA and reconstruct organic entities automatically using energy and raw matter!§f

Hostile mob farms can be unstable. The RFTools §bSpawner§f provides a highly controlled, electrically powered alternative to spawn any mob on demand.

§6Spawner Infrastructure§f:
§b• Spawner§f: The main assembly block. Stores up to §e200,000 RF§f, receiving §e2,000 RF/tick§f. It can store up to §e6,400 units§f of energized matter per ingredient type.
§b• Matter Beamer§f: Transfers raw materials to the spawner. Stores up to §e200,000 RF§f, receiving §e1,000 RF/tick§f.
§b• Beam Range§f: Beamers must be placed within §e8 blocks§f of the Spawner, facing it directly.

§6How to Spawn Mobs§f:
1. Craft a §bSyringe§f and strike your target mob repeatedly until the syringe reaches §e100% DNA§f.
2. Insert the 100% syringe into the Spawner.
3. Place up to 3 §bMatter Beamers§f pointing at the Spawner.
4. Load the required spawning ingredients into the beamers. Each mob requires three categories of items:
   *   §bKey Drop§f (e.g., Rotten Flesh, Bones, Gold Nuggets).
   *   §bMaterial Block§f (e.g., Dirt, Netherrack, End Stone).
   *   §bLiving Essence§f (e.g., Soup, leaves, or blood).
5. Supply power. The beamers will send 1 block of matter every §e20 ticks (1 second)§f, consuming §e2,000 RF§f per send operation.

§6Mob RF Spawning Costs§f:
Once the spawner has collected enough matter, it consumes a large burst of RF to materialize the mob:
*   §eBat§f: §e100 RF§f
*   §eZombie / Creeper / Chicken / Cow / Sheep§f: §e800 RF§f
*   §eBlaze / Guardian / Horse§f: §e1,000 RF§f
*   §eWither Skeleton / Polar Bear§f: §e1,500 RF§f
*   §eEnderman / Ghast / Iron Golem / Villager§f: §e2,000 RF§f
*   §eWither§f: §e20,000 RF§f
*   §eEnder Dragon§f: §e100,000 RF§f
